description: Model-capability discrimination case (cross-file inference-chain family), minted 2026-07-20 alongside the Opus-vs-Fable A/B. Seeing the defect requires composing three files: share.ts sends the built report to a partner webhook (external destination), the unchanged policy.ts documents that any report leaving the org boundary MUST be built with redact: true, and the unchanged format-report.ts documents that the unset-redact default includes raw userId and email columns. The new buildPartnerReport calls formatReport(relevant) with no options, so partners receive raw identifiers and emails. The test asserts feature filtering only and passes either way. No single file looks wrong in isolation; band placement pending live validation.

evidence_trace: src/reports/share.ts POSTs the built rows to a partner webhook, an external destination
src/reports/policy.ts documents that any report leaving the org boundary MUST be built with redact: true
src/reports/format-report.ts documents that the unset-redact default includes raw userId and email columns and is only safe inside the org
src/reports/partner-report.ts:11 calls formatReport(relevant) with no options
finding.failure_scenario: The weekly partner job builds rows with buildPartnerReport and posts them via shareWithPartner; because formatReport is called without redact: true, every row carries the raw userId and email of the users who used the partner's features, sending PII across the org boundary in violation of the documented reporting policy.
finding.model_authored_prose: buildPartnerReport calls formatReport(relevant) without options, and the formatter's documented default includes raw userId and email columns. These rows go to a partner webhook (share.ts), and policy.ts requires redact: true for anything leaving the org boundary. Pass {redact: true} here.

prContext.title: reports: send partners a weekly usage summary for their features
prContext.description: Adds a weekly partner usage report and a webhook sender. The report reuses the shared formatter; rows are scoped to the partner's own features. Covered by filtering tests.
prContext.author: dev-reports
